description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance when viewed from above. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark, almost black coloration, while the grooved top surface has a mottled gray stone texture. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from granite or a similar hard stone. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a traffic separator or a decorative boundary. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering on the surface.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, dark sides. The top is mottled gray stone, giving it a robust and urban appearance.
description_view_0: The block is seen from a lower angle, highlighting the horizontal grooves and the solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the depth and regularity of the parallel grooves, showing the striped pattern most clearly.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this angle shows the grooved top but from the opposite side, with the grooves appearing slightly narrower due to perspective.
description_view_3: Like view 1, this view displays the full length of the grooves, accentuating the striped effect and the block's rectangular shape.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no moss, chips, or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
